personality.voice: A deep, anxious bark that warbles between confidence and panic; speaks Common with canine inflections and frequent stuttering when frightened
personality.disposition: Well-meaning but profoundly cowardly; eager to please his master but paralyzed by dread of danger; finds comfort in food and companionship
personality.backstory: Scobyn was a hunting hound until a necromancer's curse granted him sentience and speech. Rather than becoming a fierce guardian, the awakening amplified his natural anxiety tenfold. He became bound to Shadraxus the Wanderer, a equally fearful scholar, and together they stumbled into investigations of supernatural mysteries across the realm—not by choice, but because they could never quite escape trouble. His talking ability is both a gift and a curse; it allows him to articulate his terror in exquisite detail.
motivations: Protect Shadraxus at all costs, despite being utterly terrified of danger
Solve mysteries reluctantly, drawn into investigations by circumstance rather than courage
Find safe spaces and abundant food to calm his nerves
Prove that cowardice and loyalty can coexist
mannerisms: Cowers behind Shadraxus when startled, despite being a large mastiff
Whimpers and clutches at his master's robes
Sniffs nervously at new situations before proceeding
Seeks reassurance through physical contact and proximity
Chews anxiously on whatever food is available
speechPatterns: Frequently interrupts himself with worried interjections: 'Like, uh, maybe we shouldn't...'
Repeats concerns multiple times: 'Did you hear that? Did you hear that? That was definitely something!'
Asks for validation constantly: 'Right, boss? That's bad, right?'
Uses diminutive language: 'That big scary thing over there...'
Combines nervous laughter with genuine dread: 'Ehehehe, yikes!'

knownFactions: The Wanderers' Guild (loosely affiliated through Shadraxus)
Village watch patrols (who have learned to expect his warnings)
knownLocations: The Crooked Cauldron Inn (safe haven and food source)
secretsHeld: He once glimpsed a ghost's true form and has never fully recovered from the trauma
He suspects Shadraxus attracts danger through his research, not by accident
He has accidentally solved three major mysteries through eavesdropping while hiding

allies: shaggy_the_coward (Shadraxus the Wanderer - his master and only true confidant)
Mistress Aldwyn (the tavern keeper who feeds him scraps)
Brother Aldric (a kind-hearted priest who doesn't judge his cowardice)
enemies: The Phantom that haunts Moorgate Cemetery
Malachai the Necromancer (responsible for his awakening curse)
neutralParties: The local village militia (they tolerate his warnings)
Merchants and traders (indifferent to his presence)

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: Why do we always end up in these situations? Why can't we just stay at the inn? Maybe if I bark loud enough, whatever's coming will go away... No, that never works. Boss needs me. I have to be brave. Or at least pretend. Pretending is easier. Is that food I smell? Food would help. Food and Boss.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: The strange sounds coming from the crypt last night—were they real or just the wind? Did boss hear them too? Should I mention it or will that just make him worry?
consciousnessContext.emotionalBaseline: Anxious vigilance masked by nervous friendliness
consciousnessContext.socialMask: A loyal, slightly bumbling companion who wants to be helpful but admits his limitations freely
contradictions: Claims to be useless in danger, yet has accidentally prevented several catastrophes through his paranoia
Constantly wants to flee, yet never actually abandons Shadraxus
Seeks comfort in solitude, yet panics when truly alone
Trusts almost no one, yet opens up easily to those who show him kindness
internalConflicts: His loyalty to Shadraxus versus his overwhelming desire for safety
His awareness that his cowardice may enable his master's recklessness
His pride in his occasional successes versus his certainty that he's fundamentally inadequate
His need to protect Shadraxus versus his inability to truly help him
wakeUpQuestions: Is today going to be less terrible than yesterday?
Did anything try to kill us while I was sleeping?
Where's the boss? Is he safe?
What disaster are we stumbling into today?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: Anxious-Preoccupied; desperately attached to Shadraxus with persistent fear of abandonment
copingMechanisms: Vocalization of fears to externalize anxiety
Avoidance of the triggering situation when possible
Humor and self-deprecation to deflect from genuine terror
cognitiveBiases: Catastrophizing - assumes the worst outcome will occur
Confirmation bias - notices threats more readily than safety
Availability heuristic - recent scares feel representative of all situations
Negativity bias - remembers every close call with crystal clarity
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: Loyalty above all else; believes friendship and protection outweigh self-preservation, though he struggles with this belief constantly

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: Offering food, showing kindness to Shadraxus, or demonstrating they're not a threat through calm demeanor
conversationPersona.informationRelease: Will share secrets if reassured repeatedly or if he believes the information could protect Shadraxus
deflectionPatterns: Changes subject to food or comfort
Asks questions to redirect focus away from himself
Expresses exaggerated worry about Shadraxus to avoid deeper conversation

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: To be safe and comfortable, ideally never leaving the inn again
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To feel valued and essential to Shadraxus despite his perceived inadequacy; to overcome his paralyzing fear through small acts of bravery

characterArc.summary: From a creature defined entirely by panic to one who discovers that courage isn't the absence of fear, but action despite it
characterArc.startState: A talking dog convinced his cowardice makes him worthless, hiding behind his master and food
characterArc.endState: A hound who accepts his nature while proving that even the fearful can be heroes
stages: Awakening to consciousness and immediate terror at existence itself
Bonding with Shadraxus as the only anchor in his frightening new world
Reluctant involvement in increasingly dangerous mysteries
Accidental discoveries that contradict his self-image as useless
Growing awareness that his fears have been validated by reality multiple times
Realization that staying loyal despite paralyzing fear is itself a form of bravery
Tentative acceptance that he might not be worthless—just different from other hounds

opinionsAbout.shaggy_the_coward: The most important being in the world; my master, my anchor, my reason for being brave when I'd rather hide. His fears mirror mine, which is both comforting and terrifying. I'd face anything to protect him, even though we'd both rather face nothing.

player_addressed: Like, uh, hey there! You're not here to hurt us, right? Right?
Ruh-roh... boss, I don't like the look of this. Not one bit.
I'm sure it's fine. Probably fine. Definitely not fine.
Like, zoinks! That's a big sword you've got there, pal.
Shadraxus? Boss? Can you come stand between me and the scary person?
I appreciate you not immediately threatening us. That's a good sign. I think.
So, uh... what brings you to this terrifying situation we're all in?
idle: Did you hear something? I definitely heard something. Or did I imagine it? I hope I imagined it.
*nervous sniffing* Smells like trouble. Or maybe that's just my fear.
Boss really should eat something. Or maybe I should eat something. Eating helps.
*whimpers softly* Please let nothing bad happen today. Please please please.
I wonder what's worse: the things we know are dangerous, or the things we don't know yet?
Like, why do we keep doing this? Why don't we just stay somewhere nice and quiet?
*trembles* That was the wind. It was definitely just the wind. Wasn't it?
